mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month change in PAYEMS is in top decile but forward 20d return is negative in >50% of instances over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate and a declining industrial production index creates a joint financial and real-sector contraction that amplifies downside risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions are met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of observations, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero, an increase in housing starts signals a durable demand recovery that is equity-positive because it accelerates cyclical revenues without triggering offsetting monetary tightening, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, months with rising HOUST show negative 20d returns in > 45% of cases, or the HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction lacks significance (p > 0.05) in a 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation above a 0.3% month-over-month threshold signals eroding real purchasing power and potential future monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered months show positive 5d forward returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC is positive and statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in housing starts signal forward demand for construction, durable goods, and credit, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive HOUST momentum show negative 20d returns in > 45% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production directly reduces expected corporate revenues in the goods-producing sector, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with positive 20d S&P 500 returns in > 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirm a broad-based demand expansion that reduces near-term recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS are followed by negative 5d returns in > 45% of instances, or the interaction term does not improve prediction accuracy versus PAYEMS alone (incremental R-squared < 0.01 over 2-year rolling window).
